text: The video displays an ICG fluorescent angiogram of an ilioanal J pouch in situ. Two loops of ileum are stapled side-to-side, with the apex of the pouch joined to the anorectal stump, following a total colectomy and proctectomy.

Indocyanine Green is injected, resulting in intense fluorescence uptake in the ileal aspect of the J pouch. The video displays three views: a standard white light view, a near-infrared fluorescence view, and a real-time augmented reality view with superimposed near-infrared over white light. The images demonstrate excellent vascular perfusion of the ileal pouch, indicating no tension on the pouch with the anastomosis in situ.

The laparoscope pans around the abdomen, revealing fluorescence in the retroperitoneum and liver due to Indocyanine Green (ICG) uptake. The liver shows persistent fluorescence as ICG is excreted into the bile.

The endoscopic view shows the internal aspects of the ilioanal pouch using a near-infrared laparoscope placed via a sigmoidoscope. There is evident fluorescence on the mucosal surface, particularly along the staple line of the side-to-side ilioilial anastomosis and the anoilial circular anastomosis.
